title: 2 Overlooked Oil Stocks to Buy Now Before They Soar
author: James Halley
description: Occidental Petroleum and Ardmore Shipping are highlighted as undervalued oil sector stocks poised for growth. Occidental has aggressively reduced debt to $13.3 billion, achieved record Permian Basin production, and maintains a $38/barrel break-even price. Ardmore Shipping benefits from elevated tanker rates driven by geopolitical disruptions, with Q1 earnings up 314% year-over-year and a newly doubled dividend payout ratio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong operational performance with record Permian production (1.43 million BOE/day), significant debt reduction ($20B to $13.3B), 306% EPS growth, $1.7B Q1 free cash flow (+52% YoY), low $38/barrel break-even, and Berkshire Hathaway backing providing downside protection. Dividend increased 8.3% for fifth consecutive year.

Strong operational performance with record Permian production (1.43 million BOE/day), significant debt reduction ($20B to $13.3B), 306% EPS growth, $1.7B Q1 free cash flow (+52% YoY), low $38/barrel break-even, and Berkshire Hathaway backing providing downside protection. Dividend increased 8.3% for fifth consecutive year.

Exceptional Q1 earnings growth of 314% YoY, revenue up 18.8%, tanker rates significantly above break-even ($52,100 spot rates vs. $11,700 break-even), dividend increased 333% to $0.39/share (3.43% yield), strong balance sheet (0.17 debt-to-equity ratio), and fleet modernization plans. Trading at less than 5x forward earnings.
Mentioned as a major stakeholder in Occidental Petroleum (27% ownership) providing downside protection and validation, but not the primary focus of the investment thesis.

title: Ardmore Shipping Provides Update on Fleet Investment, Dividend Policy, and Vessel Sale
author: Prnewswire
description: Ardmore Shipping announced it has signed contracts for two 40,500 dwt IMO2 product/chemical tankers at $44.9 million per vessel with deliveries scheduled for late 2028, with options for two additional vessels. The company is doubling its dividend payout ratio to two-thirds of adjusted earnings effective 1Q 2026 and agreed to sell a 2014-built MR tanker for $35.5 million with June 2026 delivery. Q1 2026 spot MR tanker rates averaged $33,700/day, with Q2 2026 rates improving to $50,000/day at 50% fixed.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is executing a balanced capital allocation strategy with fleet modernization (ordering two new efficient vessels), increased shareholder returns (doubling dividend payout ratio), and opportunistic asset sales. Strong Q2 2026 spot rates ($50,000/day) demonstrate improving market conditions and operational strength. The company is well-positioned with a modern fleet and integrated business model.

title: Dry Bulk, Gas (LNG/LPG), and Tanker Shipping Industry Leaders Presenting at the 20th Annual Capital Link International Shipping Forum - Monday, March 9, 2026, New York City
author: Capital Link, Inc.
description: Senior executives from leading dry bulk, gas (LNG/LPG), and tanker shipping companies will present at the 20th Annual Capital Link International Shipping Forum on March 9, 2026, in New York City. The event features panel discussions on shipping market trends, energy transition, global trade, ship finance, and strategic innovation, with participation from 23 major shipping companies and keynote remarks from U.S. Department of Energy and Greek maritime officials.

title: Ardmore (ASC) Q2 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
author: Conference Call Participants
description: Ardmore Shipping Corporation reported strong Q2 2025 earnings of $9 million, driven by favorable market dynamics in the tanker shipping sector, strategic vessel acquisitions, and improved financial flexibility through debt refinancing.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q2 earnings of $9 million, increasing TCE rates, strategic vessel acquisitions, successful debt refinancing, and positive market outlook for product tanker segment
